import cv2, math
from PIL import Image, ImageDraw
video='/app/zork.mp4'
cap=cv2.VideoCapture(video)
count=int(cap.get(cv2.CAP_PROP_FRAME_COUNT) or 0)
fps=cap.get(cv2.CAP_PROP_FPS) or 30
length=count/fps if fps else 0
samples=16
frames=[]
for i in range(samples):
    t=length*i/max(samples-1,1)
    cap.set(cv2.CAP_PROP_POS_MSEC, t*1000)
    ok, frame=cap.read()
    if not ok: continue
    frame=cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
    img=Image.fromarray(frame).resize((320,240))
    draw=ImageDraw.Draw(img)
    draw.rectangle((0,0,90,18), fill=(0,0,0))
    draw.text((4,2), f'{t:5.1f}s', fill=(255,255,255))
    frames.append(img)
cap.release()
cols=2
rows=math.ceil(len(frames)/cols)
out=Image.new('RGB',(320*cols,240*rows),(0,0,0))
for idx,img in enumerate(frames):
    out.paste(img,((idx%cols)*320,(idx//cols)*240))
out.save('/app/contact.jpg', quality=85)
print('saved',length,count,fps)
